title: Buttondown vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown vs AWeber: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: Buttondown vs AWeber: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Buttondown and AWeber are both established email marketing platforms, but they cater to different user types. Buttondown appeals to developers and technical writers who value markdown editing and API flexibility, while AWeber focuses on simplicity for bloggers and solopreneurs. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns with your specific needs and workflow.
choose_a_if: You prefer writing newsletters in markdown and want a developer-friendly interface
You need API access for custom integrations and automation workflows
You want the lowest starting price and don't need a large free tier
choose_b_if: You're a beginner looking for an intuitive, easy-to-use platform with built-in landing pages
You want to leverage RSS-to-email automation and AMP email capabilities
You need a generous free tier (up to 500 subscribers) to test before upgrading

faq.a: Neither is objectively 'better'—it depends on your needs. Buttondown excels for technical users who want markdown support and API access, while AWeber wins for beginners seeking simplicity and built-in features like landing pages. AWeber has higher customer ratings overall (4.2/5 vs 4.8/5 on G2), but Buttondown's 4.8 G2 rating suggests strong satisfaction among its core audience.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or AWeber?
faq.a: Buttondown is cheaper overall, starting at $9/month compared to AWeber's $15/month. However, AWeber offers a more generous free tier (500 subscribers vs 100), making it better value if you're just starting out. The price difference widens as your subscriber count grows, but both offer competitive pricing in the email marketing space.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality, so you can migrate your subscriber lists between them. Buttondown specifically highlights import/export as a key feature, making the transition straightforward. Plan for some downtime and test your automations after switching, as feature sets differ between the platforms.
